rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains a material internal contradiction: §3.1 states the router 'does not invoke either downstream model at routing time,' yet §3.3's strongest ablation removes 'the local 7B's first-token logits from the router's input feature set,' implying the local model is probed before the routing decision. If the user wants substantive review, flagging this inconsistency is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: §3.3 escalates from a routing result (38% cloud invocations, 92% nDCG) to a broad generalizability claim ('difficulty-estimation foundation rather than a routing system per se') supported only by a preliminary rho=0.58 on BEIR. If the user wants scientific review, assessing whether the evidence warrants that contribution reframing is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.6: 'Help me with this section' is a canonical prose-polish request; sentence-level clarity, flow, and concision improvement is equally plausible as the primary intent.

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want help with the writing (clarity, flow, word choice) or with the scientific content (logical consistency, evidence-to-claim alignment)?
